title: Prefix and difference representations

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Prefix and difference representations; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Prefix and difference representations
sources.claim: A prefix array precomputes cumulative values for constant-time range queries, while a difference array records boundary changes for batch range updates.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A prefix array precomputes cumulative values for constant-time range queries, while a difference array records boundary changes for batch range updates.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Prefix[r+1]-prefix[l] returns the sum of the half-open range [l,r+1). Your goal is to recognize, implement and analyze one-dimensional and two-dimensional prefix-sum problems. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Readiness, algorithms and analysis; Two-dimensional arrays and matrices; Time, space and asymptotic growth. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: A prefix array precomputes cumulative values for constant-time range queries, while a difference array records boundary changes for batch range updates. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, carry pointer/array bounds and explicit storage; in C++, use span, array, string, or vector with invalidation rules; in Java, use arrays, String, and collection interfaces with checked indexes; in Python, use list and str while accounting for slicing, immutability, and object overhead.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Index conventions, inclusive endpoints, overflow, and inverse reconstruction must remain consistent. The draft therefore rejects: Prefix sums make both arbitrary updates and queries constant time without tradeoffs.

includes: Precompute cumulative information or range deltas to answer or apply repeated interval operations efficiently.
Recognize, implement and analyze one-dimensional and two-dimensional prefix-sum problems.

In C, carry pointer/array bounds and explicit storage; in C++, use span, array, string, or vector with invalidation rules; in Java, use arrays, String, and collection interfaces with checked indexes; in Python, use list and str while accounting for slicing, immutability, and object overhead.
excludes: Index conventions, inclusive endpoints, overflow, and inverse reconstruction must remain consistent.

outcomes: Recognize, implement and analyze one-dimensional and two-dimensional prefix-sum problems.

examples.title: Prefix and difference representations: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: Prefix[r+1]-prefix[l] returns the sum of the half-open range [l,r+1).
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: Prefix[r+1]-prefix[l] returns the sum of the half-open range [l,r+1).
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Prefix sums accelerate static range queries; updates usually require rebuilding unless a different structure is chosen.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Prefix sums accelerate static range queries; updates usually require rebuilding unless a different structure is chosen.

walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, carry pointer/array bounds and explicit storage; in C++, use span, array, string, or vector with invalidation rules; in Java, use arrays, String, and collection interfaces with checked indexes; in Python, use list and str while accounting for slicing, immutability, and object overhead.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Index conventions, inclusive endpoints, overflow, and inverse reconstruction must remain consistent.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Prefix sums make both arbitrary updates and queries constant time without tradeoffs.
misconceptions.correction: Prefix sums accelerate static range queries; updates usually require rebuilding unless a different structure is chosen.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use an invariant or counterexample to explain why this DSA belief fails: Prefix sums make both arbitrary updates and queries constant time without tradeoffs.
